title: Simulation of smooth models of potentials with singular point using Many-Interacting-Worlds Method
abstract: The deterministic many-interacting-worlds method proposed in 2014 showed potential among the numerous interpretation of quantum mechanics. The successful application of this method in harmonic oscillator has been promoted for a long time. In this article we continue the idea about using this method to solve some bounded systems different from harmonic oscillator potential and extend to 2 dimension cases. We focus on the potential with singularity like coulomb potential and finite trap potential by some asymptotic smooth method. The numerical simulation mainly based on the dynamical algorithm proposed in many-interacting-worlds method will be used to approach the stationary states of given systems. Our results shows the consistency to the matrix Numerov method in standard quantum mechanics in solving bounded systems and provides the possibility to solve more complex systems.
summary: This paper extends the Many-Interacting-Worlds (MIW) method to simulate quantum systems with singular potentials, such as the Coulomb potential. By replacing these singularities with asymptotically smooth models, the authors demonstrate that their dynamical algorithm yields results consistent with established quantum mechanical solvers. This advances the applicability of the MIW framework to more complex, realistic physical models.
main_problem: The paper addresses the challenge of applying the Many-Interacting-Worlds (MIW) method to solve bounded quantum systems, especially those involving singular potentials like the Coulomb potential.
main_result: The MIW dynamical algorithm successfully reproduces results consistent with standard quantum mechanics methods (like the Numerov method) when singular potentials are replaced by asymptotically smooth models.
method: The core method is the MIW dynamical algorithm, which simulates the system's evolution using an interacting 'worlds' approach. This is supplemented by kernel estimation for density approximation and replacing singular potentials with smooth asymptotic forms.
model_or_system: The study focuses on solving the time-independent Schrödinger equation for various bounded potentials, including the harmonic oscillator, the 2D Coulomb potential, and finite trap potentials, extending the MIW method to 2D and higher dimensions.
key_observables: Stationary state energies ($E_n$), total energy convergence, and comparison of the MIW energy ($E_{	ext{miw}}$) against the standard matrix Numerov energy ($E$).
important_parameters:
The smoothness parameter ($
u$ or $\mu$) used in the asymptotic potential replacement, the time step ($\Delta t$), and the number of interacting worlds ($N$).
main_assumptions: The method assumes that potentials can be accurately approximated by smooth asymptotic forms near singularities, and that the dynamical algorithm will converge to the true stationary state energy.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the convergence of the total energy error for the 2D Coulomb potential as the smoothness parameter ($\mu$) is varied, and show the evolution of world configurations and bandwidth for both 1D and 2D systems.
paper_structure: The paper builds upon previous work on the harmonic oscillator, details the use of kernel estimation for density, introduces the asymptotic smoothing technique for singular potentials, and validates the dynamical algorithm's convergence against established quantum mechanical solvers.
why_interesting: The development and rigorous testing of a novel numerical framework (MIW) to handle complex, singular potentials in quantum mechanics is highly relevant for developing robust computational tools for AMO physics simulations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

(a): The evolution of N=20 worlds configuration of smooth model V (x) = erf(2(x −1)) −erf(2(x + 1)) + 2 with ∆t = 5 × 10−2 and total 40000 steps in minimum effective region [−4, 4].
